mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is rising, expected real rates are falling, liquidity conditions are loose, and equity discount rates compress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL increasing over the prior 3 months, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and spending, which reduces risk appetite and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE increase, or if the sign relationship vanishes in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, which lifts earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint PAYEMS and RSAFS improvements do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if triggered joint-strength periods show a negative average forward return.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly rise in INDPRO alongside stable labor conditions signals expanding real output without immediate capacity stress, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% m/m while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Rejected if quarter-ahead S&P 500 returns are not positive after the stated INDPRO/UNRATE pattern, or if the joint rule has no incremental predictive power beyond INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS increase above 1% after an extended zero-rate period tightens discounting conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if forward 20-day S&P 500 returns remain positive after the first cross above 1.0% in a tightening cycle, or if the signal is not negative across repeated threshold crossings.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts combined with soft GDP growth identify demand contraction and a falling wealth effect, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 2 months and GDPC1 is below its prior quarter
failure_condition: Rejected if the specified housing-and-GDP weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative ones, or if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series after a payroll peak signals labor-market rollover and weaker future consumption, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if the relationship disappears once revisions are accounted for.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains subdued, nominal spending growth is broadening faster than inflation, which supports revenues and risk appetite and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 0.5% m/m and CPIAUCSL inflation stays below 0.2% m/m
failure_condition: Rejected if the combined strong-sales/low-inflation condition fails to generate positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if high sales growth with subdued CPI does not outperform sales growth alone.
